input.background: An investigation is being conducted to study the possible effects on non-smokers of allowing smoking in a shared office environment. The goal is to determine which statistical test is most appropriate to identify differences in the central tendency of the samples collected under different scenarios.
input.question: Which statistical test would you employ if the investigators had measured the lung capacity on a scale of 1 to 5 (where 1 was poor and 5 was excellent) of the non-smoking office staff before smoking was banned, and on the same staff a month after a ban on smoking had been enforced?

output.answer: A Wilcoxon matched-pairs test would be suitable for these paired samples of ranked scores.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
